subject: Update Validation topic passwords for obfuscation
commit_message:
Update Validation topic passwords for obfuscation

Change the dummy password values to OBF: so that the properties files
will load correctly at application start-up.

Change-Id: I1903026ca57db460c41163e1f7dc42a02fd3c2e2
Issue-ID: AAI-2110
Signed-off-by: Leonard, Mark (ml401d) <ml401d@att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

@Mark, can I ask you to please provide cut-and-pasted output of all pomba pods successfully running and the pomba health check results as a comment to this patch. This will help to expedite the merge process.
Thanks,
Mike.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

no pomba in failed list pods and pomba health checks are OK

Patch Set 2:

Is this still relevant?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Is this still relevant?

Yes, this should go in, as otherwise according to the comments the property files will not load correctly.

Patch Set 2:

@Norm, @Phillip, @Pierre, @Yong, did we test these configurations?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> @Norm, @Phillip, @Pierre, @Yong, did we test these configurations?

Not that I'm aware... DMaaP never required credentials so config for username and password were always blank.

Patch Set 2:

@Prudence, given the current state of the Pomba project, should we abandon this patch?

Patch Set 2:

I think this patch should be merged as it enables the application to start up correctly with the default properties.

Looking at the changes, it does not looks to be low risk despite the lack of integration testing results. It can always be rolled back if it causes any problems.

Pomba is no more supported.
But based on the latest gating results, this patch does not hurt so we could merge
